boundary.core: Mozc ConverterInterface in a long-lived private sidecar
boundary.session_owner: Hazkey ImeReducer and external Protocol v2
boundary.transport: private length-prefixed protobuf between Swift and the sidecar
boundary.rejected_boundary: Mozc SessionHandler, because it would duplicate session, composer, keymap, watchdog, and commit state

safety_profile.learning: verified hard-off: session policy allowsLearning=false and zenzaiEnabled=false; helper uses enable_user_history_for_conversion=false and incognito_mode=true; no learning wrapper is used
safety_profile.secure_input: verified focused behavior: no core request and no context transmission; purge closes pipes and terminates the helper
safety_profile.handshake: verified: each new helper must answer a reading-free PING with the expected protocol and fixed dataset SHA before the first CONVERT is written
safety_profile.failure: verified: after the helper observes one CONVERT and exits before replying, that request is not replayed; composition remains editable and the next independent request respawns the helper
safety_profile.stale_candidates: verified: existing reducer generation/revision guards reject stale actions before a core call

dictionary_overlay.scope: project entries come from the session-scoped, composition-pinned Grimodex snapshot; personal and temporary entries come from an immutable thread-safe user-store snapshot; invalid, unsupported, and duplicate persisted records are filtered first-valid-wins at load, and system and project layers are rejected from the global user store
dictionary_overlay.ordering: project priority descending with stable ties, then personal and temporary store order, built-in guards, and Mozc; canonical NFC first-wins deduplication precedes the suggestion limit
dictionary_overlay.segmentation: natural conversion selects the longest exact dictionary prefix on a stable input boundary; explicit segment resize remains authoritative; empty overlays skip prefix search and non-empty overlays compute stable boundaries from one rendered map
dictionary_overlay.privacy: dictionary contents stay in the Swift process and secure-input paths return before either dictionary provider or the Mozc core is called
dictionary_overlay.evidence: unit coverage plus a real release server and Protocol v2 test with a fake sidecar verified project scope and pinning through resize round-trips, live personal CRUD stale-revision recovery, ranking, deduplication, limits, natural-prefix ownership, explicit resize, and secure no-call; the actual C++ helper remains covered by natural and resize smoke
dictionary_overlay.post_overlay_empty_path_regression: single 1500-sample release run: mean 0.661 ms, median 0.632 ms, p95 1.578 ms, helper launch count 1, cleanup failure count 0; this does not replace the prior five-cycle aggregate

s0_learning_off_expectation.completed: non-normative process-local cache callback; observed once for partial commit

learning_parity.runtime_capability: OpenSessionResult sets the additive proto3-optional persistent_learning_available field; false means conversion-only and absence means a legacy unknown capability
learning_parity.status: hazkey preparation implemented: v1 remains exact, the Mozc compatibility runner applies allowsLearning=false and audits real callbacks plus all-step pending=false; formal closure still requires an authoritative central v2 learning-disabled profile and a SHA-locked vendor copy

protocol_v2_scenarios.evidence_level: real Mozc Swift adapter plus fake Mozc core passed all 9 locked v1 visible/session scenarios and all 26 steps under an explicit local draft conversion-only policy; this is not v1 or formal v2 conformance. A separate supportsSegmentEditing=true reducer integration passed natural segmentation, resize round-trip, and partial commit; neither proves full product parity or all scenarios against the actual C++ helper
protocol_v2_scenarios.actual_helper_scope: natural conversion and segment resize smoke only
protocol_v2_scenarios.partial-commit: exactly-once reducer effect, remove prefix, then reconvert; learning is intentionally off in S0
protocol_v2_scenarios.secure-input: secure no-call and helper termination on purge
protocol_v2_scenarios.segment-editing: locked snapshot compatibility plus a dedicated production segmented reducer integration using native segments and ResizeSegment

external_protocol_v2_extension.public_protocol_addition: additive proto3-optional OpenSessionResult.persistent_learning_available capability; no action or snapshot semantic change

verification: strict bundle verifier pinned the helper size and SHA outside the manifest and rejected helpers whose ELF64 little-endian EM_X86_64 or runtime ABI contract did not match
strict import atomically published an exact content-addressed sha256 generation and verify-only passed
repeat import reused the exact generation while a tampered generation failed closed
CMake strict import and dataset-authenticated host runtime PING passed
65 packaging contract tests passed, including staged generation checks

added_coverage: active profile writer shutdown and private TMPDIR cleanup
generic project and personal dictionary candidate overlay
real Protocol v2 project scope, snapshot pinning, and live personal CRUD
empty-overlay long input fast path and fail-closed persisted user-dictionary loading

natural.reading: きょうはいしゃにいく

clean_builder.output_contract: built helper must match the fixed size/SHA, ELF interpreter, and GNU runtime symbol-version maxima before bundle publication
clean_builder.network_namespace_compatibility: Bazel system network usage profiling is disabled without weakening the outer no-network sandbox
clean_builder.restricted_no_network_run: stopped after 747 processes when an uncached zip_code_ken_all archive could not be fetched
clean_builder.status: artifact emit/import contracts are tested, but a fully clean offline build requires a complete fixed repository cache

remaining_gates: land the authoritative central composition-behavior-v2 learning-disabled profile and vendor its SHA lock; v1 partial-commit 1/1/1/0 remains unchanged
run a 100-500 item blind corpus with B1 or an immutable B2 snapshot
complete external SIGKILL recovery, Fcitx-inclusive soak, and alternating long-lived server benchmark cycles

comparison_limitations.dictionary: the product project and personal overlay is implemented, but B1 or an immutable B2 core dictionary has not been evaluated on a 100-500 item blind real-use corpus
comparison_limitations.server_process: Mozc session open still constructs two Hazkey KanaKanjiConverter instances and a HazkeySessionEnvironment, so server startup/RSS is not a clean Mozc-only versus Hazkey-only comparison
comparison_limitations.historical_process_ratio: the 6.31 ratio belongs to different backend harnesses and is not product key-latency evidence
comparison_limitations.default_decision: keep Hazkey as default; use Mozc only as the opt-in comparison implementation
